data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "No fixed (p, phi, psi, k) regime is sufficient to predict strict harness advantage; the determining factor is question-conditional alignment between the critic's discrimination signal and the specific candidate-level error structure, so strict improvement arises only when the critic's flag rate is higher on wrong-majority candidates than on correct-majority candidates within each question.", "mechanism": "For each question q, let r_w(q) = fraction of wrong candidates flagged, r_c(q) = fraction of correct candidates flagged. Harness strictly beats SC on q iff SC was wrong AND r_w(q) * n_wrong(q) > n_wrong(q) - n_correct(q) AND for all other questions q' where SC was right, r_c(q') * n_correct(q') < n_correct(q') - n_wrong(q'). The global averages (p, phi, psi) do not determine the per-question sign of (r_w - r_c); the same global averages can produce strict wins or strict losses depending on whether the critic's errors are concentrated on wrong-majority questions (helpful) or correct-majority questions (harmful). The repository's null results are consistent: even with recall=1.0 on the hard set, the flagged candidates were on questions where SC was already right, so no fixable-wrong-majority existed.", "scope": {"covers": ["question-conditional critic flag rates", "base error p in (0.4, 0.8) where SC is wrong on a nontrivial fraction", "k >= 3", "critic with flag rate correlated with candidate correctness at the question level"], "excludes": ["critic with uniform random flag rates independent of candidate correctness", "p <= 1/k (no wrong SC majorities to fix)", "claims that global precision/recall alone predict strict advantage"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Two conditions with identical global (p=0.5, phi=0.6, psi=0.5, k=5) but different per-question flag-correlation structure produce harness-vs-SC deltas that differ by less than 10 percentage points, falsifying the claim that per-question structure matters more than global averages", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Harness accuracy is NOT strictly above SC accuracy in the high per-question flag-correlation condition, despite SC being wrong on at least 30% of questions", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "In the low per-question flag-correlation condition (flags independent of correctness), harness does NOT lose relative to SC on at least 15% of questions where SC was right", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This candidate attacks the premise that a fixed regime suffices, arguing from the per-question structure. The decisive experiment uses a controlled critic (synthetic flag mask with tunable per-question correlation) to decouple global precision/recall from per-question alignment, directly testing whether the same global averages can yield both strict wins and strict losses."}
